zh.lifeSummary: 當Joseph Hills於1548年11月20日出生在英國艾可格亞特 布爾斯亞德時，他的父親James Hills23歲，母親Sara Smyth23歲。他於1576年與Sarah Smith在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1649年3月24日在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布爾斯亞德享年100歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布爾斯亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Hills
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hill
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

nameMeanings.name: Joseph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

variants.name: Josef
meanings: , Dutch, Scandinavian, and Czech spelling of   Joseph  , now also in use in the English‐speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Joseph Hills was born on 20 November 1548, in Great Burstead, Essex, England, his father, James Hills, was 23 and his mother, Sara Smyth, was 23. He married Sarah Smith in 1576, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 24 March 1649, in Great Burstead, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 100, and was buried in Great Burstead, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Joseph Hills(이)가 1548년 11월 20일 영국 에세스 그리트 버스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Hills(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Sara Smyth(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1576년 영국 에세스에서 Sarah Smith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1649년 3월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 버스테아드에서 100세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 버스테아드에 묻혔다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Smith в 1576 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 24 марта 1649 г. в местности грит бурстид, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 100 лет и был похоронен в местности грит бурстид, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
